description: Gradual increase of stress on body during exercise — foundational principle of all fitness improvement
rate: 2.5-5% weekly increase for strength; avoid >10% volume increase per week (injury risk)
tracking: Keep training log; if plateau for 2+ weeks, reassess programming
applies_to: All fitness goals: strength, hypertrophy, endurance, flexibility

description: First-aid treatment for acute musculoskeletal injuries
updated_protocol: PEACE & LOVE (2020): Protection, Elevation, Avoid anti-inflammatories, Compression, Education → Load, Optimism, Vascularization, Exercise
when_to_see_doctor: Unable to bear weight, significant swelling, deformity, numbness, no improvement in 72h

description: Maximum oxygen consumption during exercise — gold standard of cardiovascular fitness
measurement: mL/kg/min; tested via graded exercise test or estimated from field tests (Cooper 12-min run)
norms: Elite male endurance athletes: 70-85; average untrained male: 35-40; female: 10-15% lower
health_relevance: Strong predictor of longevity; low VO2max linked to cardiovascular disease and all-cause mortality

description: Increase in muscle fiber size (cross-sectional area) through resistance training
optimal_rep_range: 6-30 reps effective if taken close to failure; 8-12 'hypertrophy range' not uniquely superior
volume: 10-20 sets per muscle per week for most; higher for advanced trainees
protein: 1.6-2.2g/kg body weight per day for maximum hypertrophy
rest: Muscle protein synthesis elevated 24-48h post-training; train each muscle 2x/week optimal

injuries.IT Band Syndrome: lateral knee pain; hip weakness; foam roll, strengthen glutes, reduce mileage

injuries.Plantar Fasciitis: heel pain morning; calf stretching, supportive footwear, night splints

injuries.Runner's Knee (PFPS): anterior knee pain; quad strengthening, biomechanics assessment

injuries.Shin Splints: medial tibial stress syndrome; reduce mileage, bone stress reaction risk

injuries.Achilles Tendinopathy: eccentric heel drops (Alfredson protocol) gold standard treatment

importance: Sleep is the most powerful recovery tool; muscle protein synthesis peaks during deep sleep
sleep_debt: Each hour of lost sleep reduces performance ~5%; cumulative impairment underestimated by individuals
athletes_need: 8-10 hours for athletes; elite sports teams targeting 9+ hours
overtraining: HRV (heart rate variability) best objective marker of recovery status

types.static stretching: hold 30-60s; best after exercise; limited pre-workout performance benefit

types.dynamic stretching: controlled movement through range; optimal warm-up

types.PNF stretching: proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation; most effective for flexibility gains

types.foam rolling (SMR): self-myofascial release; temporary ROM improvements; reduces DOMS

mobility_vs_flexibility: Flexibility: passive ROM; Mobility: active controlled ROM with strength — mobility more functional
daily_routine: 10 minutes of hip mobility, thoracic spine, and ankle work prevents most common movement dysfunctions
